title: We need the ability to include or exclude comma's and decimals in number fields in a form and columns.
excerpt: We need the ability to users to input currency into a Monday.com form. Being able to place the comma/decimal when inputting the information will help to ensure the accuracy of the data being input when we are asking for …

posts.author: Ashley_Young (Ashley Young)
posts.cookedHtml:
We need the ability to users to input currency into a Monday.com form. Being able to place the comma/decimal when inputting the information will help to ensure the accuracy of the data being input when we are asking for currency.

Additionally, have the need for user to enter numbers that are 4+ digits long. These numbers are not currency, but when the numbers come into the board, the comma is placed and we can not get rid of it.

While using a text field is a work around, it is not for our current users as we do not want them to enter anything other than numbers, unless it is the comma or decimal.

Giving the creator the ability to add or remove these options would be very helpful.

posts.author: lost_gweedo (Will Strand)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve added my vote. I’ve seen this request/comment in other threads as well. There aren’t any reasonable workarounds that I can find or search for. Can’t use a text format column, because that adds a zero in front of my simple number.
